title: Sneaker-name normalization at ingest
description: Each retailer scraper produces a different sneaker name for the same shoe — Salomon=bare 'XT-6 GORE-TEX', Hype DC='Salomon XT-6 Gore-Tex', Platypus='Nike Mens Dunk Low Retro' (gender qualifier prefixed). update_supabase_daily.py upserts sneakers by exact (name, brand_id) match, so retailer-mismatch creates duplicate sneaker rows that the frontend renders as separate cards. Production DB today has 'XT-6' + 'Salomon XT-6', 'XT-6 GORE-TEX' + 'Salomon XT-6 Gore-Tex', 'Nike Air Force 1\'07' + 'Nike Mens Air Force 1 \'07' — all duplicates. Add a normalization pass: strip leading brand prefix, strip 'Mens' qualifier, normalize 'gore-tex'/'Gore-Tex'/'GORE-TEX', casefold for matching. Likely needs a (display_name, lookup_key) split so the rendered string stays nice. Depends on brand canonicalization landing first since the prefix strip uses the canonical brand.

title: Brand-name canonicalization at ingest
description: Different retailer scrapers produce different brand strings for the same brand (e.g. 'Salomon' vs 'Salomon brand Logo Large'; 'adidas' vs 'adidas Originals' vs 'adidas Performance'; 'Asics' vs 'ASICS Sportstyle'). update_supabase_daily.py upserts brands by exact name match, so today the production DB has 13 brand rows that collapse to 9 canonical brands. The duplicates split the brand filter chips in the frontend and prevent cross-retailer joins on the brand axis. Add a canonicalization step (regex layer or brand_aliases lookup table) between the scraper output and the upsert — must handle case folding, '<brand> brand Logo Large' alt-text bugs, and sub-brand suffixes (Originals/Performance/Sportstyle). Preserve original display strings where useful.
close_reason: Canonicalization layer landed. data_upload/canonicalize.py exposes canonicalize_brand(raw)->str: strips Hype DC 'brand Logo Large' alt-text artefact, drops Originals/Performance/Sportstyle sub-brand suffixes, normalizes casing via BRAND_ALIASES dict, raises ValueError on empty input. Wired into update_supabase_daily.py at the brand-upsert site. 11 tests, all green. Dry-run against the four jsons/*_products.json snapshots collapses 12 raw brand variants to 8 canonical brands. Existing duplicate rows in the live DB are left alone; xdl will backfill them once ery and s8z ship.

title: check for alignment of names in the supabase db, test different suppliers same shoe actually getting picked up in the db and not displaying differently such as having multiple cards for each show because of some spelling differences, how is this handled in the db, how is the join going to work for the db query write me an explaination and do some testing
design:
## How the join works today (schema-level)

The Supabase schema joins `sneakers ↔ colorways ↔ prices`, with retailer attached on
`prices.retailer_id` and `sneaker_sizes.retailer_id`. There is **no** retailer-scoped
copy of a sneaker — the design intent is that a single `sneakers` row represents one
physical shoe and accumulates retailer-specific data through `prices` / `sneaker_sizes`.
The frontend (`useSneakers` in `aussie-kicks-tracker/src/hooks/useSneakers.tsx`)
renders one card per `sneakers` row and aggregates retailer/price/stock info from
the embedded `colorways → prices` join. Cross-retailer comparison therefore lives
or dies on whether different retailer ingests collapse onto the same `sneakers` row.

## How the ingest decides "same shoe"

`data_upload/update_supabase_daily.py` upserts by **exact** name match:
- `brands`:     `select().eq("name", brand_data["name"])`
- `sneakers`:   `eq("name", sneaker_name).eq("brand_id", brand_id)`
- `colorways`:  `eq("name", colorway_name).eq("sneaker_id", sneaker_id)`

No casefolding, no trimming, no synonym lookup. Two scrapes producing different
strings for the same shoe each create a new row.

## Why retailer ingests do not align

Sampled from `sneaker-scout-backend/jsons/`:
- **Salomon (direct):** bare model, ALL CAPS, no brand prefix — `XT-6 GORE-TEX`,
  colorway `black/black/ftwsilver`.
- **Hype DC:** brand prefixed, Title Case — sneaker `Salomon XT-6`, colorway
  `White / Black / Dusky Orchid`. Brand string is `Salomon brand Logo Large`
  (a scraping bug — alt text of the logo image).
- **Platypus:** brand prefixed, sometimes a `Mens` qualifier — `Nike Mens Dunk
  Low Retro`, colorway `WHITE/BLACK`. Brand is bare (`adidas`, `Vans`).
- **Foot Locker:** brand prefixed, Title Case — `Nike Tuned 1`. Colorway uses
  dashes and trailing nicknames — `Black-Black-Black - BAT`.

The fields collide on three independent axes: brand string, sneaker string, and
colorway string.

## Concrete duplication in production right now

Live DB (2026-05-11) has 33 sneaker rows. A naïve normalization
(lowercase + strip non-alphanumerics + strip leading brand prefix + strip "Mens" +
collapse `gore-tex`/`Gore-Tex`/`GORE-TEX`) collapses to **30 canonical groups**.

The 3 cluster duplicates are:
| canonical | rows in DB |
|---|---|
| `salomon` / `xt 6` | `XT-6` (brand=Salomon) + `Salomon XT-6` (brand=`Salomon brand Logo Large`) |
| `salomon` / `xt 6 goretex` | `XT-6 GORE-TEX` (brand=Salomon) + `Salomon XT-6 Gore-Tex` (brand=`Salomon brand Logo Large`) |
| `nike` / `air force 1 07` | `Nike Air Force 1'07` + `Nike Mens Air Force 1 '07` (both brand=Nike) |

Brands: 13 rows → 9 canonical, with three dup groups:
- `Salomon` + `Salomon brand Logo Large`
- `adidas` + `adidas Originals` + `adidas Performance`
- `ASICS Sportstyle` + `Asics`

User-visible effect: a Salomon XT-6 buyer sees two cards on the listing page (one
backed by salomon.com.au prices, one by Hype DC prices) and the price-comparison
proposition silently breaks. The duplicate adidas brands also split the brand
filter chips.

Also surfaced (out of scope for this issue but worth filing): one Platypus row
is brand-misclassified (`Puma Mostro` filed under `brand=Nike`), and a `Test
Brand` / `Test Sneaker` / `Test Retailer` row pair is polluting prod.

## Recommendation (fixes to file as follow-ups)

1. **Brand canonicalization at ingest** — map raw → canonical via either a regex
   layer or a `brand_aliases` table. Must handle: `<brand> brand Logo Large`,
   `<brand> Originals/Performance/Sportstyle` sub-brand strings, case variants.
2. **Sneaker name normalization at ingest** — strip leading brand prefix, strip
   `Mens` qualifier, casefold for matching but preserve display string somewhere.
   Needs a `display_name` vs lookup-key split, otherwise we lose the nice
   "Nike Air Force 1" rendering.
3. **Colorway normalization** — at minimum casefold and collapse separator
   whitespace before the upsert key check; the rendered string can keep its
   original casing.
4. **Backfill / dedupe migration** — collapse the 3 existing duplicate sneaker
   clusters and 3 brand clusters; reattach orphaned colorways/prices.
5. **Fix Hype DC brand scraping bug** (`Salomon brand Logo Large`) — separate
   issue; the alt text grab is upstream of any canonicalization.

After (1)–(4), `gcg` (cross-retailer search-scraping) and `5qf` (price-drop page)
can rely on cross-retailer rows actually sharing a sneaker_id.
close_reason: Investigation complete — design writeup attached. Filed 5 follow-up issues (1oj brand canon, ery sneaker norm, s8z colorway norm, xdl backfill dedupe, c3o hypedc scraper bug) with dependency edges to 5qf and gcg.

title: Auto-detect Chrome major version for undetected-chromedriver
description: undetected-chromedriver auto-downloads the latest chromedriver, which mismatches when the installed Chrome is older (user's case: chromedriver 148 vs Chrome 145.0.7632.116, raising 'session not created' and falling back to static fixtures). Add a Chrome-version detector that reads 'google-chrome --version' / 'chromium --version' / Microsoft Edge fallback, parses the major number, and passes it to uc.Chrome(version_main=N). Honour HYPEDC_CHROME_VERSION env override.
close_reason: Closed

title: Swap hypedc to undetected-chromedriver + manual CAPTCHA fallback
description:
Live diagnostic confirmed HypeDC PDPs are gated by DataDome CAPTCHA (page source returns a 1.5 KB shell with a captcha-delivery.com iframe; title='hypedc.com'). Vanilla selenium + webdriver-manager is exactly the fingerprint DataDome catches.

Plan:
1. Add undetected-chromedriver to requirements.txt.
2. Replace hypedc/pagination_scraper.py setup_driver() with a uc.Chrome() build — UC patches the cdc_* markers, navigator.webdriver, and headless detection automatically, so we drop the manual CDP override and excludeSwitches.
3. Warm up the session once per run by visiting the homepage before any PDP — DataDome scores cookies; arriving on /products/* cold raises suspicion.
4. Detect DataDome blocks in extract_detailed_info() (page_source contains 'captcha-delivery.com' OR title is bare 'hypedc.com' with no h1). On detection in HYPEDC_HEADLESS=false mode, pause via input() and let the user solve the captcha manually — the resulting session cookie carries through the rest of the run.
notes: follow-up: chrome version mismatch on user host (sneaker_scout-t8e)
close_reason: Closed

Live Supabase upload not run here (no .env credentials in this dev sandbox). Deploy path:

which runs the scraper inside the container then invokes data_upload/run_update
on the produced JSON. Spot-check query:

should return > 0 after a successful run.

title: Hype DC scraper grabs logo alt-text as brand name
description: hypedc/pagination_scraper.py is writing brand.name = 'Salomon brand Logo Large' (and similar alt-text strings) instead of the bare brand. Confirmed in jsons/hypedc_products.json (items [4] and [7] for Salomon XT-6). The scraper is reading the alt attribute of the brand logo image rather than a brand text element. Independent of the name-alignment fixes (canonicalization can paper over it) but worth fixing upstream so future ingests don't keep injecting garbage.

title: Backfill: dedupe existing brand/sneaker rows
description: After the brand/sneaker/colorway canonicalization layers land, production already has accumulated duplicate rows that need to be collapsed. Known clusters (2026-05-11): brand-dups = (Salomon + 'Salomon brand Logo Large'), (adidas + 'adidas Originals' + 'adidas Performance'), (ASICS Sportstyle + Asics); sneaker-dups = ('XT-6' + 'Salomon XT-6'), ('XT-6 GORE-TEX' + 'Salomon XT-6 Gore-Tex'), ('Nike Air Force 1\'07' + 'Nike Mens Air Force 1 \'07'). Also drop the leftover 'Test Brand'/'Test Sneaker'/'Test Retailer'/'Test Colorway' test pollution. Write a one-shot migration that picks a survivor row per cluster, repoints child colorways/prices/sneaker_sizes to it, then deletes the loser. Run after the canonicalization features ship so re-ingest won't re-create duplicates.

title: Colorway-name normalization at ingest
description: Colorway strings vary wildly across retailers — Salomon='black/black/ftwsilver' (lowercase, no spaces), Hype DC='White / Black / Dusky Orchid' (Title Case, spaced), Platypus='WHITE/BLACK' (ALL CAPS) or 'BLACK (000)' (with code suffix), Foot Locker='Black-Black-Black - BAT' (dashes + nickname). update_supabase_daily.py upserts colorways by exact (name, sneaker_id) match, so the same physical colorway across retailers becomes multiple colorway rows. At minimum casefold and collapse separator whitespace for the upsert key; keep original string for display. Lower priority than the brand/sneaker work because the listing page groups by sneaker — a duplicated colorway shows up as two colorway entries under the same card, which is less broken than two separate cards. Still needs fixing before 5qf (price-drop page) can rank by colorway.

title: stress test, run a test that scrapes at least 3 pages of content for each sneaker, from the hypedc website
Size unit mismatch: US sizes stored as uk_size in Supabase
description:
parse_uk_size() in data_upload/update_supabase_daily.py strips the numeric value from size strings (e.g. '4 US' -> 4, '8.5 UK' -> 8.5) and stores it in the sizes table's uk_size column regardless of the actual unit. This means a US size 10 gets stored as uk_size=10, but UK 10 and US 10 are different physical sizes (UK 10 ≈ US 11).

Affected scrapers:
- platypus: sizes are 'X US' (US mens)
- hypedc: sizes are 'X US' by default (PDP has US/UK/EU tabs, scraper reads whichever is selected — defaults to US)
- salomon: sizes are 'X UK'

The sizes table has three columns (uk_size, us_size, eu_size) but only uk_size is reliably populated. us_size is set to uk_size + 0.5 as a rough approximation (line 329 of update_supabase_daily.py), which is wrong for both US-sourced and UK-sourced data.

Potential solution:
1. Have each scraper tag sizes with their unit explicitly, e.g. {"value": 10, "unit": "US"} instead of "10 US" string parsing.
2. Add a conversion table or function (US mens to UK is roughly -1, EU to UK is roughly -33.5) to normalize all sizes into uk_size, us_size, and eu_size columns correctly.
3. Update parse_uk_size() to detect the unit suffix and apply the correct conversion before storing. For example: if unit is US, store value in us_size and compute uk_size = value - 1; if unit is UK, store directly; if unit is EU, store in eu_size and compute uk_size = value - 33.5.
4. Backfill existing rows in the sizes table that were inserted with the wrong unit mapping.

Impact: the frontend's size filter and size availability display currently show misleading size labels. A user filtering for UK 10 may see results that are actually US 10 (a full size smaller).

title: Build platypus scraper mirroring hypedc structure
description: Scrape https://www.platypusshoes.com.au/shop/mens/footwear/sneakers using the same pattern as hypedc/. Use static HTML fixtures in platypus/freesh/ for selector development. Output must use singular-key format (sneaker, brand, colorway, prices, retailer, sizes) consumed by data_upload/update_supabase_daily.py. Use undetected-chromedriver with PLATYPUS_HEADLESS=false for manual CAPTCHA solving. Relative imports so 'python -m platypus.pagination_scraper' works.
notes: Built platypus/ scraper mirroring hypedc/ structure: __init__.py, static_fallback.py, single_product_page_scraper.py, pagination_scraper.py. Detail parser is JSON-LD-first (Platypus PDPs ship a Product schema block with brand/name/description/sku/price/currency/availability), with DOM fallbacks for sizes (button[data-analytics-pdp='size-select'].available), unit (US/UK/EU parsed from the CHOOSE SIZE label), colour name/code, and hero image. Listing parser keys off div.productCard with a text-link-with-dollar-sign heuristic. Pagination uses a LOAD MORE button (no ?page=N) — max_pages=N means '1 + (N-1) clicks'. PLATYPUS_HEADLESS=false enables manual bot-challenge solving. Verified against fixtures: 16 cards parsed, 17 sizes (16 available) extracted from NB 9060 detail page, output matches singular-key shape consumed by data_upload/update_supabase_daily.py. Known limitation: static_fallback slug-token matcher can mismatch when fixture filenames share generic words (e.g. 'black') — same caveat as hypedc.
close_reason: Closed

title: HypeDC PDPs return blocked/empty pages — listing OK, detail rejected
description:
Live run output: every product detail page reports 'PDP h1 didn't render in 10s' and 'Variant fieldset didn't render in 20s', producing JSON with empty description/colorway/sizes. The listing page parses 60 products fine, so this is route-specific to /products/* — most likely a Cloudflare bot challenge that listings are exempt from but PDPs aren't.

Step 1 (this issue): add diagnostics so we can see what HypeDC is actually returning for a PDP — log driver.title + current_url + first 500 chars of body text on h1-timeout, and dump the full page_source of the first failed PDP to disk for inspection.

Step 2 (next issue once we know): pick a remediation — undetected-chromedriver, longer pre-navigation cookie warm-up, scroll-to-trigger, or a different driver entirely.
close_reason: diagnostics shipped — next step depends on what the first_failure dump shows

title: HypeDC PDP hydration: extend variant-fieldset wait to 20s + h1 prerequisite
description:
First live run reported 'Variant fieldset didn't render; parsing what's there anyway' for every product, producing JSON with empty sizes/description/colorway. HypeDC's PDP is a Nuxt SSR shell that hydrates the variant grid client-side; the original 8s wait wasn't enough.

Fix: stage the wait (h1 first as a 10s SSR-shell check, then the variant fieldset for up to 20s, with a 0.5s settle for child-label paint). When the fieldset still doesn't appear, log a clearer warning so a future regression is greppable. Also fix is_available in combine_product_info: when hydration timed out and detail dict has no sizes + no price, default to True if the listing surfaced a price (the listing already proved the product exists).
close_reason: Closed

title: Card price = lowest price within active retailer + size filters
description:
Once useSneakers returns a real lowest_price (sneaker_scout-b21), make sure that price respects the active filter set. Right now (post-b21) lowest_price would be the global cheapest across all retailers/sizes for a sneaker. When the user has filtered to e.g. retailer X + size 9, the displayed price should be min(prices.price) joined to sneaker_sizes where retailer_id IN selectedRetailerIds AND size_id maps to one of selectedSizes — i.e. cheapest among the survivors of the filter, not the global min.

Edit the price subquery added in b21 to apply the same retailer/size constraints already applied to the colorway join. Add a small subtitle on SneakerCardNew like 'from $X · N retailers' so users know the price is filter-scoped, and so a sneaker whose only matching retailer is expensive doesn't look like a great deal at a glance.

Depends on sneaker_scout-b21 (real prices).
close_reason: lowest_price now scoped to retailer + size filter intersect: when size filter is active, build eligibleCwRetailer set from sneaker_sizes survivors and exclude prices whose (colorway, retailer) pair isn't in it. Added retailer_count field on Sneaker and 'across N retailer(s)' subtitle on SneakerCardNew. Verified via Playwright on /?sizes=4: 3 sneakers shown with prices, each labelled 'across 1 retailer' (Salomon-only DB). XT-6 GORE-TEX correctly drops its 'aspargusgreen' colorway when size 4 isn't available there. Logic also handles multi-retailer case correctly though current Salomon-only data can't visually demonstrate price difference. Unblocks igp.

title: Retailer button on SneakerDetail opens per-product URL (prices.product_url)
description:
At src/pages/SneakerDetail.tsx:422 the external-link button opens retailer.website_url — a generic store homepage. The DB already has prices.product_url (per retailer, per colorway — see src/integrations/supabase/types.ts:125). The button should deep-link to the actual product page so users land on the sneaker they were viewing.

Update fetchRetailersForSneaker in src/lib/sneakerQueries.ts:144 to select product_url alongside price/colorway_id. Pass it through to the visibleRetailers array. Have the click handler open product_url || retailer.website_url (fall back when null, which is fine for retailers we haven't scraped per-product URLs from yet).

Verify by clicking through on a Salomon sneaker — should land on a Salomon product page, not the homepage.
close_reason: fetchRetailersForSneaker now selects prices.product_url and carries it into the retailerMap alongside the chosen price. SneakerDetail external-link button opens product_url || website_url. Verified live: clicking the link on AERO GLIDE 4 detail page captured https://salomon.com.au/collections/mens/products/aero-glide-5 (product page) instead of the homepage https://salomon.com.au — the per-product URL the scraper already populated is now actually used.

title: Replace mock price/stock fields in useSneakers with real DB queries
description:
At src/hooks/useSneakers.tsx:118-120 the hook fabricates lowest_price (Math.random*200+100), price_change (Math.random*40-20), and in_stock (Math.random>0.3). Every consumer of these fields — the price on SneakerCardNew, the In Stock and Price Drops stats on Index.tsx, the on-sale tab — is therefore meaningless.

Replace with real values: lowest_price = min(prices.price) where is_available=true (scoped to active retailer filter when set); in_stock = any sneaker_sizes.is_available=true matching active size+retailer filters; price_change = delta of the two most recent price_history rows per colorway. The reference logic already exists in src/lib/sneakerQueries.ts:fetchAllSneakers but is per-sneaker (N+1 queries). Lift it into useSneakers and batch: one prices query, one sneaker_sizes query, one price_history query for the page's colorway IDs, then reduce in JS.

Supersedes sneaker_scout-2he (which scoped only price_change indicators).
close_reason: Replaced random Math.random() mock for lowest_price/in_stock/price_change in useSneakers with three batched DB queries (prices, sneaker_sizes, price_history) — N+1 collapsed to 3 round trips per page. Verified via Playwright: cards now show real Salomon prices ($259.99-$369.99) instead of random 100-300; size=4 filter narrows to 3 in-stock; size=13 keeps all 7. SneakerCardNew also updated to handle undefined lowest_price gracefully. Unblocks 60q/k6i/4ha.

title: Sidebar size filter is hardcoded [7-12] whole numbers; missing half-sizes and edge sizes
description: FilterSidebar.tsx:15 hardcodes sizeOptions=[7,8,9,10,11,12]. The sizes table actually contains 17 distinct US sizes including halves and edges (4-13). A user wanting to filter by US 9.5 or US 13 has no UI affordance. Fix: add a useSizes hook (parallel to useBrands/useRetailers) returning sorted distinct us_size values, render all of them in the sidebar grid.
close_reason: Added useSizes hook (queries distinct us_size from sizes table). FilterSidebar now renders all 17 DB sizes (US 4 through US 13 incl. halves) instead of hardcoded [7-12]. Validated via Playwright: previously unreachable US 9.5 now filters to 4 sneakers.

title: Make size units consistent: detail page shows UK while sidebar uses US
description: The Available Sizes panel on SneakerDetail renders sizes as '8.5 UK' etc. (reads sizes.uk_size in SneakerDetail.tsx ~201,212), while the listing-page sidebar filter shows 'US 7'-'US 12' and the size filter URL/state is in US. A user who filters by 'US 9' lands on the detail page and sees '8.5 UK' — same physical size, different labels, jarring. Fix: swap detail page to render us_size so the whole UI speaks US consistently.
close_reason: Swapped Available Sizes panel on SneakerDetail to render us_size instead of uk_size, matching the listing sidebar. Validated via Playwright (sizes now display US 8, US 8.5, ..., US 13).

title: Filter prices panel in SneakerDetail by active retailer filter
description: When a retailer filter is active (selectedRetailerIds in FilterContext is non-empty), the SneakerDetail prices panel should only show matching retailers. Read selectedRetailerIds from FilterContext and filter the retailers array before rendering. Add a small inline notice near the panel ('Filtered by N retailer · Clear') so users know filtering is active. Depends on FilterContext existing (sneaker_scout-t2b).
close_reason: SneakerDetail prices panel now filters retailers via selectedRetailerIds from FilterContext. Inline notice shows 'Filtered by N retailer(s) · Clear' (link clears the filter). Cheapest-retailer auto-select constrained to visible retailers. Validated via Playwright (matching id, non-matching id empty state, clear).

title: Add quick supplier filter access from SneakerDetail page
description: Users should be able to adjust retailer filters from within the single shoe page without navigating back. Implement compact inline retailer toggle pills near the prices panel that read from and write to FilterContext. Each pill shows the retailer name and toggles its selection state. Depends on FilterContext existing (sneaker_scout-t2b).
close_reason: Added retailer toggle pills above the Retailer Prices panel on SneakerDetail. Each pill reads/writes selectedRetailerIds via toggleRetailer; aria-pressed reflects state, active pill is filled blue. Validated via Playwright: pill click adds ?retailers=... to URL, second click removes it.

title: Add Pydantic models mirroring Supabase canonical schema
description:
The Python backend has dataclasses in utils/models.py that model the scraper's intermediate shape (Product, ProductSize, DetailedProduct), but no canonical type for the Supabase tables the frontend actually reads. The uploader (data_upload/update_supabase_daily.py) currently builds dicts by hand against the table shape, with no validation.

Add a Pydantic module modelling brands, sneakers, colorways, prices, sneaker_sizes, price_history, sizes, retailers, plus a composite SneakerWithPrices type. Mirror the shape from aussie-kicks-tracker/src/integrations/supabase/types.ts so Python and TS agree. Add pydantic to requirements.txt. Leave the existing dataclasses alone — they model scrape-time scratch space and have three importers.
close_reason: Closed

title: Auth-gate /admin (follow-up to admin page)
description:
Once /admin exists (sneaker_scout-jwl) and is shipped public, lock it down. Simplest path: use the existing useAuth hook in src/pages/Admin.tsx — when no user, redirect to /auth?next=/admin and return after login. No role check yet — any signed-in user can see admin stats.

If a stricter role check is wanted later, that's a separate decision (would need a profiles.is_admin column or similar). File this as a low-priority follow-up so /admin can ship and be validated first.

Depends on sneaker_scout-jwl (the page must exist before it can be gated).
close_reason: Closed

title: Add 'split colorways' toggle on listing grid (Grouped default, Split opt-in)
description:
Different colorways of the same sneaker can have different lowest prices and different stock states across retailers. Today the listing groups colorways under one card showing only the primary colorway's image and a single price — a colorway with a steeper discount or unique availability gets hidden.

Add a toggle in the listing header (next to grid/list view buttons) that switches between:
- **Grouped** (default — current behaviour): one card per sneaker, primary colorway image, swatch row showing siblings.
- **Split**: one card per colorway. Each card carries that colorway's own image and its own filter-scoped lowest_price (depends on sneaker_scout-60q). Click navigates to /sneaker/:id?colorway=<colorwayId>; SneakerDetail reads the query param and calls setSelectedColorway to preselect.

Persist the toggle in URL (?view=split) so it survives reload and is shareable. Default stays Grouped — least disruptive to existing users.

Depends on sneaker_scout-60q (need colorway-level lowest_price for split cards to be meaningful).
close_reason: Closed

title: Move stats + Price Trends to /admin; show Total Sneakers + Total Colorways on Index
description:
The In Stock and Price Drops tiles plus the Price Trends panel on Index.tsx are admin-y noise for the public listing — the user wants them moved to a dedicated /admin page once they show real data (sneaker_scout-k6i, sneaker_scout-4ha).

Steps:
1. Create src/pages/Admin.tsx and add <Route path='/admin' element={<Admin />} /> in App.tsx.
2. Move the In Stock / Price Drops cards from Index.tsx:71-93 to Admin.
3. Move the Price Trends Card block at Index.tsx:104-131 to Admin, and replace the three hardcoded mockPriceData PriceCharts (Nike Dunk / Air Jordan / Yeezy — not even brands we have!) with real data: top three sneakers ranked by recent price drop magnitude (or by price_history activity), each rendered as a PriceChart fed from price_history rows.
4. On Index, replace the 3-tile row with a 2-tile row: Total Sneakers (existing count) and a new Total Colorways tile. Add a useColorwaysCount hook beside useSneakers that counts colorways whose sneaker_id survives the active filter.

/admin ships PUBLIC for now — auth gating is filed separately as a follow-up.

Depends on sneaker_scout-k6i and sneaker_scout-4ha (no point moving fake stats).
close_reason: Closed

title: Real In Stock stat (count over full filtered set)
description:
Index.tsx:35 does inStockSneakers = sneakers.filter(s => s.in_stock).length. Same two problems as Price Drops: pre-b21 in_stock is random, and post-b21 the count would still only cover the current page.

After sneaker_scout-b21, replace with an aggregate query: count distinct sneakers that have at least one sneaker_sizes row with is_available=true matching the active retailer+size filter. Surface as the 'In Stock' tile (which after sneaker_scout-NEW5 lives on /admin).

Depends on sneaker_scout-b21.
close_reason: Added useInStockCount and usePriceDropsCount hooks issuing aggregate queries over the full filter-scoped set. useInStockCount uses count:exact head:true on sneakers with inner-join through colorways→sneaker_sizes (is_available=true, plus retailer/size when filtered). usePriceDropsCount fetches matching sneaker+colorway IDs, then their price_history (desc), then computes per-sneaker latest-vs-prior delta in JS and counts negatives. Index.tsx tile values now come from these hooks instead of page-level filter; the 'On Sale' tab keeps a page-scoped count (priceDropsOnPage) since its contents are also page-scoped. Verified live: unfiltered shows 7/7/0; ?sizes=4 narrows to 3/3/0; direct DB call confirms count=7. Both unblock jwl.

title: Real Price Drops stat (count over full filtered set)
description:
Index.tsx:36 currently does priceDrops = sneakers.filter(s => (s.price_change || 0) < 0).length, which (a) is random mock pre-b21, and (b) only counts the current page even after b21 lands.

After sneaker_scout-b21 wires real price_change, replace this with an aggregate count over the *full filtered set* (not just the current page of 20). Issue a separate count query that joins price_history twice (most-recent and second-most-recent per colorway/retailer) and counts sneakers where the delta < 0 across all retailers/sizes the active filter allows. Surface as the 'Price Drops' tile (which after sneaker_scout-NEW5 lives on /admin).

Depends on sneaker_scout-b21.
close_reason: Added useInStockCount and usePriceDropsCount hooks issuing aggregate queries over the full filter-scoped set. useInStockCount uses count:exact head:true on sneakers with inner-join through colorways→sneaker_sizes (is_available=true, plus retailer/size when filtered). usePriceDropsCount fetches matching sneaker+colorway IDs, then their price_history (desc), then computes per-sneaker latest-vs-prior delta in JS and counts negatives. Index.tsx tile values now come from these hooks instead of page-level filter; the 'On Sale' tab keeps a page-scoped count (priceDropsOnPage) since its contents are also page-scoped. Verified live: unfiltered shows 7/7/0; ?sizes=4 narrows to 3/3/0; direct DB call confirms count=7. Both unblock jwl.

title: Implement real price change indicators on listing cards
description: The price_change value in useSneakers hook is a random mock - the TrendingUp/TrendingDown arrows on listing cards are meaningless. Replace with real calculation: query price_history for the two most recent entries per colorway per retailer, compute the delta, and surface as a real signed price_change value. Also fix the Price Drops stat in Index.tsx to count real changes. Additionally, guard the detail page price history arrow to only render when real priceHistory rows exist. This is deprioritised - do after higher-priority issues.

value: Old Supabase migration file at aussie-kicks-tracker/supabase/migrations/20250716112750-*.sql still seeds Nike/Adidas/Jordan/etc dummy data — would re-introduce dummy sneakers if re-run on a fresh DB. (Production state evolves quickly; do not assume only one brand exists — check the live DB.)

src/lib/sneakerQueries.ts has TWO versions of sneaker-listing logic: the lib function fetchAllSneakers (legacy, N+1, only consumed by /test page) and the live hook useSneakers (in src/hooks/). When changing listing behaviour, edit the hook — the lib function is dead in production paths.

Sneaker/brand/colorway names are NOT canonicalized at ingest. data_upload/update_supabase_daily.py upserts by exact-string match on (brand.name) → (sneaker.name, brand_id) → (colorway.name, sneaker_id). Each retailer scraper produces a different naming convention (Salomon=bare ALLCAPS, Hype DC=brand-prefixed Title Case + 'Salomon brand Logo Large' bug, Platypus=brand-prefixed sometimes with 'Mens', Foot Locker=dash-separated colorways), so the same physical shoe lands as multiple sneaker rows. Live DB as of 2026-05-11: 33 sneakers reduces to ~30 canonical groups under simple normalization. Filed follow-ups (z1t investigation): brand canonicalization, sneaker-name normalization, colorway normalization, dedupe/backfill migration, Hype DC alt-text brand scraper bug.

useSneakers in /workspace/aussie-kicks-tracker/src/hooks/useSneakers.tsx now exposes per-colorway pricing (lowest_price/in_stock/retailer_count/price_change on each colorway entry) for the split listing view. Per-sneaker aggregates remain on the sneaker object. The split view (?view=split) renders ColorwayCard, the grouped (default) renders SneakerCardNew.

/admin route is implemented in src/pages/Admin.tsx and is auth-gated: redirects to /auth?next=/admin when no user. Auth.tsx routes back to ?next= on success (path validated to start with '/' and not '//' to prevent open-redirect).

hypedc scraper at sneaker-scout-backend/hypedc/. Three modules: pagination_scraper.py (entry: python -m backend.hypedc.pagination_scraper, ?page=N URL pagination, max_pages/max_products_per_page=3 default), single_product_page_scraper.py (parse_detail_html for tests + extract_detailed_info for live), static_fallback.py (parses freesh/ fixtures, returns None on miss). Selenium-init failure or page-1 load failure routes to static fallback so dev/CI runs always produce some output. Output JSON shape matches salomon_products.json exactly (singular keys).

Platypus Shoes PDPs ship a JSON-LD Product schema block in the initial HTML — that's the cleanest source for brand, name, description, sku, color, price, currency, availability. The DOM is needed for the size grid (button[data-analytics-pdp='size-select'] with .available class) and the size unit ('CHOOSE SIZE' label area shows 'US MENS' / 'UK' / 'EU'). The JSON-LD model[] array is unreliable (duplicated names) — don't use it for sizes.

Platypus listing uses a LOAD MORE button (button text 'LOAD MORE'), not URL-based pagination. Initial render is ~60 cards; total ~566. Each click expands the grid in place, doesn't navigate. Listing card selector is div.productCard; each card has multiple <a href='.../slug.html'> children — the one with text content + a $ price is the brand/name/price link, others wrap the image swiper.
